history.what_worked: Cross-referencing on-disk skills/ against the CLAUDE.md table found 7 missing skills; cross-referencing two sections of CLAUDE.md against scripts/install-skills.sh found a contradictory Gemini install path.
history.what_didnt: Trusting CLAUDE.md as a source of truth for skill paths. Line 25 said ~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/, line 70 said ~/.gemini/skills/, install script said ~/.gemini/skills/. The doc lied; the script was right.
history.rule_change: When auditing CLAUDE.md in this repo, diff ls skills/ against the skills table AND diff every install path against scripts/install-skills.sh. Treat the install script as authoritative for paths.

history.what_worked: When evaluating skill descriptions in CLAUDE.md tables or SKILL.md frontmatter, scoring them against the trigger-not-summary rule surfaces concrete weaknesses ('helps with X' is a fail; 'use when the user asks to ...' is a pass).
history.what_didnt: Treating the description field as a one-line summary. Anthropic's own skill-authoring docs and multiple 2026 community guides (Babich, Pixelmojo, Composio) converge on: the description is the trigger Claude reads to decide whether to load the skill — vague triggers cause missed loads; overly broad triggers cause wrong-skill loads.
history.rule_change: When auditing a skill in this repo, evaluate its description against the trigger test: would a fresh Claude session, seeing only name+description (~100 tokens), correctly decide whether to load this skill for a plausible user request? If not, flag it. Apply the same test when reviewing the skills table in CLAUDE.md.
